findings.evidence: `rg` shows line 41 now has `variable ... {ι : Type*}` and line 47 defines `noncomputable def signPattern`; the diff shows this variable previously included `[Finite ι]` and there was no `omit [Finite ι] in` on this definition.
findings.fix: Preserve the old public signature, or introduce the generalized version under a new name and route/deprecate the old entry point.
findings.issue: `signPattern` is public and its exported constant type no longer includes the previous inherited `[Finite ι]` instance argument.

findings.evidence: `rg` shows line 168 defines `noncomputable def signHom` under the non-finite variable block at line 41; the diff shows the old file-level variable was `{ι : Type*} [Finite ι]` and `signHom` itself was not under `omit [Finite ι] in`.
findings.fix: Keep `signHom` at the old signature, or publish the generalized homomorphism under a new name with a compatibility/deprecation path for the old API.
findings.issue: `signHom` is public and likewise loses the previous inherited `[Finite ι]` argument, breaking fully explicit downstream uses of the old constant.

summary: The cleanup still silently changes public API by removing an inherited instance argument from public declarations. Private helper changes are not compatibility issues.
